gaps.title: Webhook Replay Attack Vulnerability
gaps.breaks: Unverified duplicate webhook requests could trigger unintended billing operations
gaps.scenario: An attacker captures a valid subscription webhook event and replays it multiple times, causing multiple billing charges or subscription state changes for the same event
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a replay attack on the Paddle webhook endpoint. Create a test that sends the same valid webhook payload twice with identical signatures and verify that the second request is rejected with a 409 status code. Also test that replaying an old webhook (with different timestamp) is rejected.

gaps.title: Tenant Isolation Breach in Billing Operations
gaps.breaks: One company could access another company's billing data through API calls
gaps.scenario: A malicious user with company_id=1 crafts a request to get_subscription with company_id=2, potentially exposing sensitive billing information
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in all billing operations. Create a test that attempts to access billing data from one company (company_id=1) while authenticated as another company (company_id=2). Verify that all database queries include the company_id filter and that unauthorized access returns a 403 error.

gaps.title: Missing Idempotency Keys for Subscription Operations
gaps.breaks: Duplicate subscription creation requests could result in multiple subscriptions being created
gaps.scenario: A network timeout causes a client to retry a create_subscription request, resulting in two active subscriptions for the same customer and company
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies idempotency for all subscription operations. Create a test that sends identical create_subscription requests twice with the same idempotency key and verify that only one subscription is created. Also test that sending the same request without an idempotency key results in an error.

gaps.title: Race Condition in Subscription Updates
gaps.breaks: Concurrent subscription updates could leave the system in an inconsistent state
gaps.scenario: Two administrators simultaneously try to update the same subscription - one adds more seats, the other changes the plan - resulting in a subscription state that's a mix of both changes or a complete failure
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent subscription updates. Create a test that makes simultaneous update_subscription requests for the same subscription with different parameters and verify the final state is consistent and only one update is applied. Use database transactions and locks to ensure isolation.

gaps.title: Webhook Signature Verification Bypass
gaps.breaks: Malicious actors could send fake webhook events that appear legitimate
gaps.scenario: An attacker crafts a fake subscription cancellation webhook with a properly formatted but invalid signature, causing the system to incorrectly cancel a subscription
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies the HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for all webhooks. Create test cases for: valid signature, invalid signature, missing signature, tampered payload, and correct signature but wrong payload. Ensure all invalid cases return a 401 status and don't process the webhook.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 5

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Webhook Replay Attack Vulnerability
What breaks: Unverified duplicate webhook requests could trigger unintended billing operations
Real scenario: An attacker captures a valid subscription webhook event and replays it multiple times, causing multiple billing charges or subscription state changes for the same event
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a replay attack on the Paddle webhook endpoint. Create a test that sends the same valid webhook payload twice with identical signatures and verify that the second request is rejected with a 409 status code. Also test that replaying an old webhook (with different timestamp) is rejected.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Tenant Isolation Breach in Billing Operations
What breaks: One company could access another company's billing data through API calls
Real scenario: A malicious user with company_id=1 crafts a request to get_subscription with company_id=2, potentially exposing sensitive billing information
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in all billing operations. Create a test that attempts to access billing data from one company (company_id=1) while authenticated as another company (company_id=2). Verify that all database queries include the company_id filter and that unauthorized access returns a 403 error.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Missing Idempotency Keys for Subscription Operations
What breaks: Duplicate subscription creation requests could result in multiple subscriptions being created
Real scenario: A network timeout causes a client to retry a create_subscription request, resulting in two active subscriptions for the same customer and company
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies idempotency for all subscription operations. Create a test that sends identical create_subscription requests twice with the same idempotency key and verify that only one subscription is created. Also test that sending the same request without an idempotency key results in an error.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Race Condition in Subscription Updates
What breaks: Concurrent subscription updates could leave the system in an inconsistent state
Real scenario: Two administrators simultaneously try to update the same subscription - one adds more seats, the other changes the plan - resulting in a subscription state that's a mix of both changes or a complete failure
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent subscription updates. Create a test that makes simultaneous update_subscription requests for the same subscription with different parameters and verify the final state is consistent and only one update is applied. Use database transactions and locks to ensure isolation.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Webhook Signature Verification Bypass
What breaks: Malicious actors could send fake webhook events that appear legitimate
Real scenario: An attacker crafts a fake subscription cancellation webhook with a properly formatted but invalid signature, causing the system to incorrectly cancel a subscription
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies the HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for all webhooks. Create test cases for: valid signature, invalid signature, missing signature, tampered payload, and correct signature but wrong payload. Ensure all invalid cases return a 401 status and don't process the webhook.
